headline: In Mali, the jihadists' goal 'is not taking Bamako, but changing the people in power'
description: The offensive by the Al-Qaeda-affiliated group JNIM threatens Mali's capital, which is under blockade. In an interview with Le Monde, analyst Jean-Hervé Jezequel examines the jihadists' objectives.

headline: Trump says US will hike tariffs on EU cars and trucks to 25%
Baguettes take center stage on France's Labor Day as government encourages bakers to work
In Brazil's 'slaughterhouse capital,' workers are under pressure: 'The companies want us to be robots'
description: To cope with the demand for meat, expected to increase under the EU-Mercosur agreement, slaughterhouses recruit from among Indigenous populations and immigrants. Employees report harsh working conditions, including a grueling pace, cold temperatures and faulty machines.

headline: Ruth Gruenthal, 103-year-old Holocaust survivor, reclaims her German citizenship
description: Eighty-six years after being stripped of her citizenship, the American retiree living in New York decided to have it 'restored' out of fear of Donald Trump's authoritarian tendencies.

headline: Seafood and veggie tide inundates dining tables on the Basque coast
description: Young talents focused on vegetables and fish have elevated the gastronomic offerings in Biarritz and its neighboring town Bidart, in a region traditionally passionate about meat.

headline: 'I continue to protest, but with a knot in my stomach': How Sweden is discouraging foreigners from taking climate action
description: After standing trial for participating in a protest against private jets, French national Isabelle Letellier was also denied citizenship. This double penalty highlights how Stockholm has toughened its stance toward environmental activists.

One year from presidential election, a 'quiet anger' in France's streets
In a small French village, a father is charged with sexual abuse of 34 victims aged 3 to 9
description: A tree, a rope, a letter. After surviving a suicide attempt, a man revealed he had committed numerous crimes against children. After a year-long and controversial investigation, he was charged, on April 10, with rape and sexual assault of minors and recording child pornography.
byline: Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Lorraine de Foucher

headline: 'Writing helps us organize our ideas. How will we understand the world if we let AI do the writing?'
description: Le Monde columnist Philippe Bernard explores the questions raised by the decline of writing and the rise of artificial intelligence for our cognitive abilities.

headline: The French love their public libraries. But lack of resources undermines the experience
description: France's unique network of 15,500 local cultural spaces, which are open to all, free of charge, has seen visitor numbers steadily increase, leading to certain issues.

headline: 'Céline Dion will generate the equivalent of one-fifth of the economic impact of the Paris Olympics'
